Serverless AI Endpoints Reference

Serverless AI Endpoints Reference

Section titled “Serverless AI Endpoints Reference”

Create Endpoint

Section titled “Create Endpoint”

CPU-only (with remote inference via Token Factory)

Section titled “CPU-only (with remote inference via Token Factory)”

Best for agent/orchestration workloads that don’t need local GPU:

Note: Multiple --container-port flags are supported. Port 8080 is the health check, port 18789 is the OpenClaw dashboard.

GPU (self-hosted inference)

Section titled “GPU (self-hosted inference)”

For running models locally on GPU:

With vLLM for LLM inference

Section titled “With vLLM for LLM inference”

Key Parameters

Section titled “Key Parameters”
ParameterDescription
--nameHuman-readable endpoint name
--imageDocker image (from Nebius registry or public)
--platformGPU/CPU platform (see GPU platforms table)
--presetResource allocation (e.g., 1gpu-16vcpu-200gb)
--container-portPort your container listens on (repeatable for multiple ports)
--container-commandOverride container entrypoint
--argsArguments to the container command
--disk-sizePersistent disk size (e.g., 100Gi)
--shm-sizeShared memory size (e.g., 16Gi, important for PyTorch)
--envEnvironment variables (repeatable)
--publicExpose a public URL
--auth tokenRequire auth token for access
--tokenSet the auth token value
--subnet-idVPC subnet for private networking

Manage Endpoints

Section titled “Manage Endpoints”

Update Endpoint

Section titled “Update Endpoint”

Polling for Ready State

Section titled “Polling for Ready State”

After creating an endpoint, poll until it’s ready:

Cost Considerations

Section titled “Cost Considerations”
  • CPU-only (cpu-e2): Cheapest option. Use for orchestration/agent workloads with remote inference.
  • GPU endpoints: Billed per GPU-hour while running. Always stop when not in use.
  • --public: Creates a public URL. Omit for internal-only access.
  • --auth token: Strongly recommended for public endpoints to prevent unauthorized access.
